lessons: Core bug found and fixed: the documented BOI API path (sdmx/v2/data/dataflow/BOI/EXR/1.0 and IR_INTEREST/1.0) returns 404. The working data path is ws/public/sdmxapi/rest/data/{DATAFLOW}/{series}: exchange rates are EXR/RER_<CUR>_ILS, interest is the BIR dataflow, CPI is PRI. Verified live (USD/ILS ~2.81-2.91 early June 2026). Fixed SKILL.md, SKILL_HE.md, references/boi-api.md, and the script.

Script also had two latent bugs: it parsed the wrong XML shape (generic ObsValue elements instead of the real flat <Obs TIME_PERIOD OBS_VALUE> attributes) so it always fell back to fake example data, and it used the default urllib User-Agent which the BOI edge server rejects. Both fixed; parsing validated against real API XML.
Aligned the representative-rate publication time to ~15:30 (was inconsistent: 16:00 in SKILL.md vs 15:30 in the reference). Refreshed the stale example fallback FX levels (USD 3.60 -> ~2.90).

Could not run the script end-to-end in the sandbox (intercepting proxy causes SSL CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED); verified the URL via curl and the parser against the downloaded XML. The script correctly does NOT disable TLS verification.
Next cycle: identify the single headline BOI policy-rate series within BIR (it has ~30 sub-series) so --interest can return the rate programmatically instead of pointing to the website.
